name: Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata
name_original: Belvis de Monroy desde Navalmoral de la Mata – circular por el Castillo de Monray
description: Cycle 20.1 miles (32.3 km) on this moderate route from Navalmoral de la Mata, circling the historic Castillo de Monroy with 1147 feet of gai
region_intro: Touring cycling around Navalmoral De La Mata offers routes through the diverse landscapes of Extremadura, characterized by extensive dehesas of holm and cork oaks, open plains, and pine forests. The region provides access to the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, featuring varied terrain from gentle meadows to areas with more demanding climbs. Natural features such as the Valdecañas Reservoir and the Tajo River contribute to the scenic environment for cycling. The area's topography includes rolling hills and flat sections, suitable for different touring cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Navalmoral De La Mata The most popular touring cycling route is Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route explores the local countryside and passes by historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Talayuela, a moderate 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route offers varied landscapes, including agricultural areas and open fields. Local touring cyclists also love the Charca Mayen loop from Navalmoral de la Mata, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through natural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Navalmoral De La Mata is defined by dehesas, plains, and the proximity to the Monfragüe National Park, offering a mix of natural and cultural landscapes. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Navalmoral De La Mata are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from several reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Navalmoral De La Mata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Navalmoral de la Mata, catering to various skill levels. These include 8 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 3 more difficult options, ensuring a diverse range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The terrain around Navalmoral de la Mata is diverse, featuring extensive dehesas of holm and cork oaks, open plains, and pine forests. You'll encounter rolling hills and flat sections, with some routes, particularly those near the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offering more demanding climbs and winding descents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, for example, explores local countryside with varied topography.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: Yes, there are several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. The region offers routes through gentle meadows and plains. While specific easy routes are not detailed in the provided data, 8 of the total routes are classified as easy, making them suitable for less experienced cyclists.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in the Navalmoral de la Mata area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with more significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33728120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy – Castillo de Monroy loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering over 61 km with substantial elevation gain. The Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve BTT Center also features routes with demanding climbs and descents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes often pass through the characteristic Extremaduran dehesas, with their holm and cork oaks, and offer views of the Valdecañas Reservoir, a significant bird habitat. You can also explore areas near the Monfragüe National Park, known for its wild beauty and birdwatching opportunities. Historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible along some routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or towns accessible by touring bike from Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: Yes, several historical sites and charming towns are accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical landmarks. The town of Toril, with its 15th-century urban structure and the Interpretation Center of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, is part of the cycling network. Navalmoral de la Mata also serves as a good starting point for exploring historic towns like Plasencia, Trujillo, and Cáceres, which boast Roman and Moorish influences.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The region is excellent for cycling even in winter due to its overflowing nature, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling. These seasons avoid the intense heat of summer and provide comfortable conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes of Extremadura.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in the area?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted, the presence of 8 easy routes suggests options suitable for families or those with younger riders. The Vía Verde de Monfragüe, a 'Green Way,' is known for being an accessible path, making it a good choice for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What do other cyclists think about the touring routes in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The touring cycling routes around Navalmoral de la Mata are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from several reviews. Over 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the mix of natural and cultural landscapes and the options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Navalmoral de la Mata are designed as loops, offering convenient circular journeys. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca Mayen loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and end in the same location without retracing your path.

Q: Can I access cycling routes using public transport in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: Navalmoral de la Mata serves as the closest train and bus station to the start of the Ciclosenda del Tajo, indicating good public transport access for cyclists. This makes it a convenient hub for reaching various cycling routes in the region without needing a car.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a good mix of agricultural and open field landscapes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Talayuela&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46 km path that offers varied landscapes, including agricultural areas and open fields. This route provides a good perspective on the rural character of the Extremaduran countryside.
tips: Cycling towards the city from the west, the path is lined with olive and oak trees and you will enjoy a spectacular view of the 14th century fortified Castillo de Monroy.

One of Cáseres' jewels, Castillo de Monroy is a 14th-century fortified castle that protects the trade routes along the Tagus River. Its elevated location will give you spectacular views of the surrounding landscape.
A 3.5 m granite jurisdictional landmark dating from the 14th century symbolizes the importance and governance of the city.

Large town in the north of the province of Cáseres. The town of Navalmoral de la Mata is the gateway to the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park in the northern part. Cycle through the city to Plaza de España to view heritage buildings, including the impressive Iglesia de San Andrés.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter narrow alleys, sections through holm oaks and cork oaks, and dehesas (wooded pastures). Be prepared for unpaved sections with ruts, loose stones, and sand, which can make parts of the route quite technical. While some parts may be paved, significant portions are dirt roads.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for all types of touring bicycles?
faqs.answer: While classified as a touring bicycle route, the trail's challenging terrain, including steep gradients (up to 19%), loose stones, and sand on unpaved sections, makes it more suitable for mountain bikes or robust gravel bikes. Traditional touring bicycles, especially with heavy loads, might find certain sections very difficult.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll get a great View of Castillo de Monroy, and cycle by the impressive Castillo de Monroy itself. Other historical landmarks include the Jurisdictional Pillar of Belvís de Monroy and the historic Convent of San Francisco. The starting point in Navalmoral de la Mata also features the Plaza de España and Iglesia de San Andrés.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Navalmoral de la Mata. You can typically find parking within the town, especially near the Plaza de España, which is a key landmark at the beginning of the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best time to cycle this route is during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness specifically for this cycling route is not explicitly stated. However, generally, dogs are permitted on many trails in rural Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always best to check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle here?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It passes through public areas and roads.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a wonderful landscape characterized by Mediterranean forest and dehesas, which are unique ecosystems in Europe. This area is also appealing to ornithologists, so keep an eye out for various bird species. The route offers picturesque views of the Extremaduran countryside.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Camino de Monfragüe - Etapa 4, Camino de Monfragüe - Etapa 5, Camino del Canal, Avenida de Extremadura, Calle Garganta Alardos, and Sendero Multiusos.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Navalmoral de la Mata, the starting and ending point of the circular route, offers various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Belvís de Monroy also has some local establishments where you might find refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the technical sections and varied terrain, it's advisable to pack appropriate gear. This includes a helmet, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (especially for punctures), and sun protection. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are recommended. If you're using a touring bicycle, consider if your tires are robust enough for loose surfaces.
